preview_text:
The model rates Iran as a clear favorite with a 65% win probability, compared to New Zealand's 10% chance. This 55-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial disparity in the forecast. Iran enters as the heavy pick, though a New Zealand upset remains within the tails of the distribution.

The expected scoreline spread reveals a competitive match despite the favorite's edge. Draw probability sits at 25%, suggesting regular-time parity is a meaningful outcome. The 40-point gap between Iran's win chance and New Zealand's indicates Iran is favored but not overwhelming; the match shape leans toward decisive outcomes but with a sizable contingent of level results plausible.

The model and market diverge notably here. Sportsbooks assign Iran 52% and New Zealand 20%, compressing the model's 55-point favorite margin to roughly 32 points. The model sees Iran as significantly stronger; the market treats the tie tighter. Polymarket prices align closely with sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better captures the true competitive balance.
